chunks.text: Mengjie Yu | EECS at UC Berkeley Mengjie Yu: Faculty Home Page Larger Photo Mengjie Yu Assistant Professor Contact Information 508 Cory Hall mengjie.yu@berkeley.edu Office Hours 9:30-10:30am Wed, 508 Cory Links Personal Homepage Research Support Catie Anders cmanders@berkeley.edu Biography I am accepting students! Read about how to apply. Mengjie Yu is an Assistant Professor of Electrical Engineering and Computer Sciences and Weber Family Engineering Faculty Fellow at the University of California, Berkeley. She received her B.A. in Optical Engineering from Zhejiang University in 2012, and her Ph.D. degree in the School of Electrical and Computer Engineering in 2018 from Cornell University. She held a research staff associate position in the Department of Applied Physics and Mathematics at Columbia University from 2015-2018. From 2018 to 2021, she was a postdoctoral fellow at the School of John A. Paulson School of Engineering and Applied Sciences at Harvard University. Before joining Berkeley, she was Gabilan Assistant Professor in the Department of Electrical and Computer Engineering at the University of Southern California. She serves as an associate editor for NPJ Nanophotonics, guest editor for Nanomaterials, Photonics Research and Optical Material Express, and on the Early Career Editorial Advisory Board for APL Photonics and Advanced Photonics. She is the committee chair of the Conference on Lasers and Electro-Optics (CLEO), Micro and Nano Photonics Devices and Photonics North, committee member of the Advanced Photonics Congress, Optical Fiber Communication (OFC) conference, Frontiers in Optics (FiO), IPR and ACP conferences. She is the 2020 Optica (formerly OSA) Ambassador,

Photonics. She is the committee chair of the Conference on Lasers and Electro-Optics (CLEO), Micro and Nano Photonics Devices and Photonics North, committee member of the Advanced Photonics Congress, Optical Fiber Communication (OFC) conference, Frontiers in Optics (FiO), IPR and ACP conferences. She is the 2020 Optica (formerly OSA) Ambassador, the Caltech 2019 Young Investigator Lecturer, and the Rising Star Women in Engineering in the Asian Deans Forum 2019. She served as chair of the OSA Integrated Photonics Technical Group from 2019 - 2021. She was a recipient of the DARPA Young Investigator Award in 2023, the Powell Faculty Research Award in 2022, and the Maiman Award and Emil Wolf Award in 2016. Education 2018, Ph.D, Electrical and Computer Engineering, Cornell University 2012, B.A, Optical Engineering, Zhejiang University Research Areas Physical Electronics (PHY) Artificial Intelligence (AI) Integrated Circuits (INC) Micro/Nano Electro Mechanical Systems (MEMS) Teaching Schedule EE 134.
